title: [security] Inconsistent constant-time comparison for pubkey TOFU in link layer
description:
Reviewed commits touching schnorr48.c (4ab0fc434), coap_keys.c (854678569, 3999ab592), lichen-coap/codec.rs (ba9b24653 and related), coap_config.c, rpl/trickle.c and merges.

Key findings:
- Good: Addition of key_ct_compare() in coap_keys.c:375 (volatile OR of XORs) used for IID lookup and TOFU pubkey pinning. Matches pattern in replay.c:public_key_ct_compare().
- Schnorr48 verify_frame uses crypto_verify16 (Monocypher ct) + s_nonzero_acc OR reduction. Domain separation comments cleaned.
- CoAP codec restored InvalidPayloadMarker per RFC 7252 - prevents malformed packet parsing issues that could affect OSCORE.
- Issue: lichen_l2.c:773, link/link_ctx.c:280, app_identity.c:218, lichen_l2.c:773 still use memcmp() for public_key/ed25519_pk in TOFU pinning and peer lookup (see LICHEN spec 8.6). These paths (peer_add on RX frames, key load) are security-sensitive for key handling/crypto timing. Inconsistent with recent coap/replay fixes. Could enable timing attacks to distinguish pinned vs new keys.
- No obvious OSCORE nonce reuse, EDHOC missing in recent changes, MIC verify has dangerous Kconfig opt but documented. No key material logged.
- No EDHOC changes in last 20 commits.

Recommend adding unified ct_compare util and replacing all secret key compares. OSCORE/EDHOC key derivation should be audited next.
close_reason: Replaced memcmp with crypto_verify32 (Monocypher constant-time compare) in all TOFU and peer pubkey checks in app_identity.c, lichen_l2.c, link_ctx.c, and announce.c. Matches existing patterns in replay.c, coap_keys.c, edhoc.c. Commit 373917582
